## Top Stories

### 1. NHS AI blood test could reduce invasive womb cancer checks

**Source:** AI News  
**Link:** [https://www.artificialintelligence-news.com/news/nhs-ai-blood-test-womb-cancer-checks/](https://www.artificialintelligence-news.com/news/nhs-ai-blood-test-womb-cancer-checks/)

HEADLINE: NHS Introduces AI Blood Test to Streamline Womb Cancer Assessments

SUMMARY: Several NHS hospitals are set to implement an AI-driven blood test designed to evaluate women suspected of having womb cancer prior to conducting invasive examinations. This initiative aims to reduce the number of invasive procedures among the approximately 90,000 postmenopausal women referred annually in England due to heavy bleeding.

KEY POINTS:
- The AI blood test will assist in the preliminary assessment of women referred for womb cancer checks.
- Approximately 90,000 postmenopausal women are referred each year, with about 10,000 diagnosed with womb cancer.
- The use of AI technology could significantly decrease the need for invasive procedures.
- NHS hospitals are leading the charge in integrating AI into routine diagnostic practices.

IMPACT: This development could revolutionize cancer diagnostics by enhancing patient care, minimizing unnecessary procedures, and optimizing healthcare resources through the integration of AI technology.

### 2. NVIDIA Releases Nemotron-Labs-3-Puzzle-75B-A9B: A Compressed Hybrid MoE LLM Delivering 2.03x Server Throughput at Matched User Throughput

**Source:** MarkTechPost  
**Link:** [https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/07/09/nvidia-releases-nemotron-labs-3-puzzle-75b-a9b-a-compressed-hybrid-moe-llm-delivering-2-03x-server-throughput-at-matched-user-throughput/](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/07/09/nvidia-releases-nemotron-labs-3-puzzle-75b-a9b-a-compressed-hybrid-moe-llm-delivering-2-03x-server-throughput-at-matched-user-throughput/)

HEADLINE: NVIDIA Unveils Nemotron-Labs-3-Puzzle-75B-A9B: Enhanced Hybrid MoE LLM with 2.03x Throughput

SUMMARY: NVIDIA has introduced the Nemotron-Labs-3-Puzzle-75B-A9B, a compressed version of its Nemotron-3-Super model, achieving significant improvements in server throughput thanks to innovative hardware-aware structural compression techniques. The new model reduces its parameters while dramatically increasing concurrency capabilities, making it more efficient for high-demand applications.

KEY POINTS:
- The model reduces total parameters from 120.7B to 75.3B and active parameters from 12.8B to 9.3B.
- It offers 2.03 times the total throughput of the previous Super model at a consistent user throughput of 100 tokens per second.
- Concurrency capabilities increase from 1 request to 8 on an H100 with 1M-token requests.
- Utilizes a hybrid MoE (Mixture of Experts) architecture for optimized performance.

IMPACT: This advancement enhances the efficiency of AI models, enabling businesses to handle more simultaneous requests, which is crucial for scaling AI applications in real-time environments.

### 3. Google AI Studio Adds Import from GitHub to Build a Deployable App

**Source:** MarkTechPost  
**Link:** [https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/07/09/google-ai-studio-adds-import-from-github/](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/07/09/google-ai-studio-adds-import-from-github/)

HEADLINE: Google AI Studio Introduces GitHub Import Feature for App Development

SUMMARY: Google AI Studio has launched a new feature allowing users to import projects directly from GitHub into its Build mode. This functionality converts existing repositories into a runtime-compatible format, enabling developers to easily iterate on their projects and deploy applications more efficiently.

KEY POINTS:
- New "Import from GitHub" feature in Google AI Studio enhances app development workflow.
- Converts existing GitHub repositories into a format suitable for runtime compatibility.
- Users can iterate, deploy, and further develop their applications seamlessly.
- Aimed at streamlining the transition from code repository to deployable app.

IMPACT: This development significantly streamlines the app deployment process for developers, fostering rapid innovation and reducing time-to-market for AI-driven applications.

### 4. Robbyant Releases LingBot-VLA 2.0: An Open-Source 6B Vision-Language-Action (VLA) Model for Cross-Embodiment Robot Manipulation

**Source:** MarkTechPost  
**Link:** [https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/07/08/lingbot-vla-2-0/](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/07/08/lingbot-vla-2-0/)

HEADLINE: Robbyant Unveils LingBot-VLA 2.0: A Groundbreaking Open-Source Model for Advanced Robot Manipulation

SUMMARY: Ant Group's Robbyant has launched LingBot-VLA 2.0, an open-source vision-language-action model designed for cross-embodiment robot manipulation. This 6B model is pretrained on extensive data, allowing it to map various robotic embodiments into a unified action space, significantly enhancing its performance on generalist benchmarks.

KEY POINTS:
- LingBot-VLA 2.0 utilizes a 6B checkpoint pretrained on 60,000 hours of data, including 50,000 hours of robotic trajectories.
- It features a token-level, auxiliary-loss-free Mixture-of-Experts action expert to optimize capacity without loss balancing.
- The model incorporates dual-query distillation techniques for improved geometric and temporal supervision.
- It surpasses previous iterations and benchmarks, including π0.5 and LingBot-VLA-1.0, in performance evaluations.

IMPACT: This advancement in cross-embodiment robotic manipulation could lead to more sophisticated and versatile robots capable of performing complex tasks in various environments, driving innovation in automation and robotics industries.

### 5. SpaceXAI Releases Grok 4.5, a Cursor-Trained Model for Coding, Agentic Tasks, and Knowledge Work at $2/M Input

**Source:** MarkTechPost  
**Link:** [https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/07/08/spacexai-releases-grok-4-5/](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/07/08/spacexai-releases-grok-4-5/)

HEADLINE: SpaceXAI Unveils Grok 4.5: A Cutting-Edge Model for Coding and Knowledge Work

SUMMARY: SpaceXAI has launched Grok 4.5, a Cursor-trained model designed for coding, agent-based tasks, and knowledge work applications. With a processing capability of 80 transactions per second and a competitive pricing structure of $2 per million tokens, Grok 4.5 also ranks first on Harvey's Legal Agent Benchmark, indicating its strong performance in legal applications.

KEY POINTS:
- Grok 4.5 is specifically engineered for coding and agentic tasks.
- It operates at 80 transactions per second (TPS).
- The model is priced at $2 per million tokens, with an option for $6 per million.
- It holds the top position on Harvey's Legal Agent Benchmark.
- The release emphasizes the growing demand for efficient AI models in professional sectors.

IMPACT: The introduction of Grok 4.5 signifies a notable advancement in AI capabilities for coding and knowledge work, potentially transforming how businesses approach automation and legal tasks.

### 6. Netflix AI Team Cuts Wide-Partition Read Latency from Seconds to Milliseconds by Splitting Cassandra Partitions Per ID

**Source:** MarkTechPost  
**Link:** [https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/07/08/netflix-ai-team-cuts-wide-partition-read-latency-from-seconds-to-milliseconds-by-splitting-cassandra-partitions-per-id/](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/07/08/netflix-ai-team-cuts-wide-partition-read-latency-from-seconds-to-milliseconds-by-splitting-cassandra-partitions-per-id/)

HEADLINE: Netflix Engineers Achieve Millisecond Read Latency by Optimizing Cassandra Partitioning

SUMMARY: Netflix's engineering team has successfully reduced read latency from seconds to milliseconds by implementing innovative partitioning strategies in Apache Cassandra. Utilizing a combination of time slice re-partitioning and dynamic partitioning based on TimeSeries ID, the team has enhanced data accessibility while maintaining the availability of large partitions.

KEY POINTS:
- Implemented two approaches: Time Slice re-partitioning for future partitions and dynamic partitioning for oversized partitions.
- Detection of large partitions is conducted through byte counting and Kafka.
- Partition splits undergo checksum validation, while Bloom filters help route reads to parallel child partitions.
- Average read latency improved significantly to low double-digit milliseconds.
- 500MB+ partitions remain accessible during the process.

IMPACT: This advancement enhances Netflix's data handling efficiency, potentially leading to improved user experiences and more robust performance in data-intensive applications.

### 7. Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic

**Source:** AI News & Artificial Intelligence | TechCrunch  
**Link:** [https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/08/googles-deepfake-detector-system-used-to-debunk-mcconnell-hoax-pic/](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/08/googles-deepfake-detector-system-used-to-debunk-mcconnell-hoax-pic/)

HEADLINE: Google’s Deepfake Detector Successfully Identifies McConnell Hoax Image

SUMMARY: A recent incident involving a fabricated photo of Kentucky Senator Mitch McConnell in a hospital bed highlighted the growing prevalence of AI-generated misinformation. Google’s deepfake detection technology was employed to debunk the image, underscoring the importance of advanced tools in combating disinformation in the digital age.

KEY POINTS:
- An AI-generated image falsely depicted Senator Mitch McConnell in a hospital setting, sparking false narratives.
- Google's deepfake detection system effectively identified the image as a hoax, showcasing the tool's capabilities.
- This incident emphasizes the rising threat of AI-generated misinformation and the necessity for reliable detection methods.
- The use of such technology is critical for maintaining public trust and integrity in media.

IMPACT: The successful identification of the deepfake demonstrates the critical role of advanced AI detection tools in safeguarding truth and combating the spread of misinformation in today’s media landscape.

- This innovation could lead to a significant leap in robotic capabilities and applications in various industries.
